item.text: I’ll execute the single turn from the provided briefing only, choosing Zap for immediate damage/tempo and pushing units toward uncontested centers.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'set -euo pipefail
RUN=.games/e024-codex-rush-vs-engine-guided
cat > "$RUN/actions/turn-001.deck.json" <<'"'JSON'
{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-001\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":[{\"type\":\"playAction\",\"handIndex\":4},{\"type\":\"moveToBuy\"},{\"type\":\"endTurn\"}]}
JSON
bun run --silent cli -- deck-turn --config rulesets/territory-v1-cost6-damagecap-responsewin-lead4-highmove-center6/deck.yaml --state \""'$RUN/deck.json" --actions "$RUN/actions/turn-001.deck.json" --result "$RUN/results/turn-001.deck.result.json"
cat > "$RUN/actions/turn-001.board.json" <<'"'JSON'
{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-001\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":{\"movements\":[{\"unit\":\"P1-raider-start-1\",\"from\":{\"col\":10,\"row\":1},\"to\":{\"col\":8,\"row\":1}},{\"unit\":\"P1-scout-start-2\",\"from\":{\"col\":11,\"row\":0},\"to\":{\"col\":8,\"row\":3}},{\"unit\":\"P1-raider-start-3\",\"from\":{\"col\":12,\"row\":1},\"to\":{\"col\":8,\"row\":3}},{\"unit\":\"P1-marksman-start-4\",\"from\":{\"col\":11,\"row\":1},\"to\":{\"col\":9,\"row\":2}}],\"recruits\":[],\"attacks\":[],\"heals\":[],\"upgrades\":[]}}
JSON
bun run --silent cli -- board-turn --state \""'$RUN/board.json" --deck-result "$RUN/results/turn-001.deck.result.json" --actions "$RUN/actions/turn-001.board.json" --result "$RUN/results/turn-001.board.result.json"
bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run "$RUN" --deck-result "$RUN/results/turn-001.deck.result.json" --board-result "$RUN/results/turn-001.board.result.json" --summary "P1 played Zap for immediate damage pressure, skipped a low-impact buy, and advanced aggressively toward the center." --reasoning "RUSH plan: convert the opener into damage tempo with Zap, avoid spending the buy on non-pressure filler, claim the northeast center immediately, and move the remaining units forward to contest central supply next turn." --strict-win
bun run --silent validate-run -- --strict --strict-deck --strict-win "$RUN/timeline.json"
printf '"'\\nDECK_RESULT\\n'
node -e 'const r=require(\"./.games/e024-codex-rush-vs-engine-guided/results/turn-001.deck.result.json\"); console.log(JSON.stringify({phase:r.phase, active:r.active?.id, actions:r.actions, buys:r.buys, money:r.money, attributes:r.attributes, gained:r.gained, trash:r.trash, winner:r.winner}))'
printf 'BOARD_RESULT\\n'
node -e 'const r=require(\"./.games/e024-codex-rush-vs-engine-guided/results/turn-001.board.result.json\"); console.log(JSON.stringify({supply:r.supply, supplyControl:r.supplyControl, winner:r.winner}))'"

Deck turn complete: .games/e024-codex-rush-vs-engine-guided/results/turn-001.deck.result.json

item.text: The deck step completed. The board validator rejected one move because two units landed on the same hex, so I’m keeping the same aggressive shape and moving the second raider to an adjacent forward hex instead.

item.text: The turn committed and strict validation passed. I’m reading only the generated result files now so the final deck and board lines reflect the actual committed output.

Committed and validated `turn-001`.

Deck line: played `zap`, moved to buy, ended turn. Bought nothing. Produced `8` money and `1` damage.

Board line: moved `P1-raider-start-1` to `(8,1)`, `P1-scout-start-2` to `(8,3)`, `P1-raider-start-3` to `(8,2)`, and `P1-marksman-start-4` to `(9,2)`. No recruits or attacks.

Rationale: Zap gives immediate damage tempo, and the board move claims/pressures the northeast center while advancing the whole opening group toward central supply control. Strict validation passed.
